India vs South Africa 2nd Test Day 1 Live Updates: Playing XI & Toss Details

Nov 24, 2025

The India vs South Africa 2nd Test, Day 1 began at the Barsapara Cricket Stadium in Guwahati, with South Africa winning the toss and choosing to bat first. After a tough defeat in Kolkata, India are keen to bounce back and level the two-match series.

Toss and Early Batting

South African captain Temba Bavuma won the toss and opted to bat on a pitch expected to assist spinners as the day progresses. Aiden Markram and Ryan Rickelton opened the innings for the visitors.

India, led by their newly appointed Test captain Rishabh Pant, took the field with confidence. Pant noted that despite losing the toss, India were eager to bowl first and exploit early conditions on the Guwahati surface.

India’s Playing XI Changes

India made two key changes to their XI following the first Test:

  • Nitish Kumar Reddy replaces the injured Shubman Gill, who remains sidelined due to a neck issue.
  • Sai Sudharsan comes in for Axar Patel to strengthen the batting lineup.

Pant, India’s 38th Test captain, emphasized that both changes were made to maintain balance and adapt to Guwahati’s pitch conditions.

Impact of Shubman Gill’s Absence

Gill’s absence is a major setback for India’s top order. However, Pant expressed confidence in India’s bench strength, highlighting the squad’s depth and readiness to step up when needed.

South Africa Line-up Adjustments

South Africa also made a single change to their playing XI:

  • Senuran Muthuswamy replaces Corbin Bosch.

Bavuma’s team will look to make the most of the toss advantage and put up a competitive first-innings total.

Key Players to Watch

India: Rishabh Pant (captain), Mohammed Siraj, Ravindra Jadeja, Nitish Kumar Reddy, Sai Sudharsan.
South Africa: Temba Bavuma, Aiden Markram, Ryan Rickelton, Kagiso Rabada.

Live Updates and Match Situation

Fans tuned in to the India vs South Africa 2nd Test live can follow updates via television broadcasts and streaming platforms. South Africa’s openers aim to build a solid platform, while India’s bowlers are focused on early breakthroughs.

Day 1 will reveal how the pitch behaves and how both teams shape their innings strategy as the Test progresses.

Conclusion

The second Test is crucial for India as they seek to level the series after a disappointing loss in Kolkata. With Rishabh Pant at the helm and strategic changes in the playing XI, India will look to assert themselves early. Fans can follow live updates, ball-by-ball coverage, and expert analysis throughout Day 1 for comprehensive match insights.
